hub_subtitle: Inglês para Trabalho Remoto em Tecnologia & Quality Assurance

lesson.menu_title: A Day in QA -- Describing Your Routine
lesson.menu_desc: Describing your typical workday and what you are working on right now, to a remote teammate -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- A Day in QA
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 3 -- A Day in QA

listenings.text: Hi, and welcome to the QA team. Let me explain how a normal day works here. Every morning, we start with a fifteen-minute stand-up. Each tester gives a short update: what they finished yesterday, what they are working on today, and any blocker. After that, I open the backlog and I prioritize the bugs. The critical ones always come first. Then I assign each ticket to one tester, and we track the progress on the same board all day. We work in two-week sprints, so we plan on Monday and we review on Friday. One thing matters most here: when you find a critical bug, you report it right away. Clear communication keeps the whole team moving.

Hi Ana, it is Sofia. A quick update on where we are right now. At the moment, I am testing the new login feature, and it is looking good so far. The developers are fixing two bugs from yesterday, so I am waiting for the new build before I continue. Meanwhile, I am writing the test cases for the payment screen. The client is joining our call at three today, so I am preparing a short summary of our progress. I am not blocked, but I am a little behind, so I might need your help this afternoon. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, Ana joins the fifteen-minute morning stand-up and gives her update. Then she opens the backlog and prioritizes the critical bugs. Next, the lead assigns each ticket to one tester. After that, Ana tests the feature she is handling today and tracks the progress. Finally, at the end of the day, the team reviews what they have finished.
